Texts.Home:Title: Welcome!
Texts.Home:Description: This sample demonstrates ABP's URL-based localization feature.
Texts.Home:TryIt: Try switching the language above and notice how the URL changes.
Texts.About:Title: About This Sample
Texts.About:Description: URL-based localization embeds the culture code directly in the URL path, for example /tr/about or /en/about.
Texts.About:HowItWorksDetail: ABP uses ASP.NET Core's built-in RouteDataRequestCultureProvider. When a request arrives at /{culture}/page, the culture segment is captured by routing and the current thread culture is set accordingly.
Texts.About:MiddlewareOrderDetail: The only configuration requirement is that UseAbpRequestLocalization() must be called after UseRouting().
Texts.About:ApiRoutes: API routes (e.g., /api/...) are not affected — they have no {culture} segment so the provider returns null and falls through to the next provider.
